scores.reasoning: The homepage https://front.com describes Front as a customer service platform with shared inbox and customer communication features, matching the description given.
The cited evidence URL https://dev.front.com/docs/authentication failed to fetch, so no direct evidence supports the claim about API key and OAuth2 authentication methods.

The help article https://help.front.com/en/articles/2331 confirms that personal API tokens can be created by company admins via settings without mention of paid plans or human approval, supporting self_serve_free.
The developer portal https://dev.frontapp.com shows extensive REST API documentation and references, supporting the claim of a broad REST API surface.
The combination of public docs, self-serve token creation, and API references indicates the API is buildable today with no main blockers, consistent with the note.
